sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Cagliari are struggling, having failed to win in their last 7 matches, with only 1 draw and 4 losses in their last 5 games. Genoa, on the other hand, have shown slight improvement with a win and a draw in their last 5 matches, though they remain winless in 3 consecutive away games. Both teams are in the lower half of the Serie A table, with Cagliari at 14th (10 points) and Genoa at 18th (7 points).

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Recent head-to-head encounters between Cagliari and Genoa have been tightly contested, with their last two meetings ending in draws (1-1 and 2-2). Over the last 5 matchups, results are balanced with 1 win each and 3 draws, suggesting another close battle is likely.

sections.subtitle: Team Statistics
sections.text: Cagliari have struggled offensively, scoring only 9 goals this season (14th in Serie A), while conceding 14 (12th). Genoa are similarly lackluster with 8 goals scored (18th) and 16 conceded (among the worst in the league). Cagliari's low corner count (2.9 per game) and Genoa's high foul rate could influence set-piece and disciplinary outcomes.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: Sebastiano Esposito stands out for Cagliari with an average Sofascore rating of 7.35 and 3 goals in head-to-heads against Genoa. For Genoa, Mikael Ellertsson has contributed recently with a goal and could be a threat in midfield.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sofascore's prediction of a 51% chance for a home win, combined with Cagliari’s slightly better position and home advantage at Unipol Domus, they edge out as favorites. However, Genoa’s resilience in recent head-to-heads and Cagliari’s winless streak suggest a draw remains a strong possibility. Expect a low-scoring, gritty encounter.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Cagliari and Genoa at the Unipol Domus ended in a thrilling 2-2 draw. Both teams showcased their attacking intent, with goals coming from Vitinha and Leo Østigård for Genoa, and Gennaro Borrelli and Sebastiano Esposito for Cagliari. The scoreline reflects an open game, with defensive lapses on both sides allowing for a flurry of goals, particularly in the closing stages of the half. With the game evenly poised, the second half promises more excitement as both teams push for a winner.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Cagliari's first half performance
halftimeSections.text: Cagliari demonstrated dominance in possession and territorial control during the first half. They created a fair amount of pressure in the final third, frequently entering the opposition’s penalty area. Their attacking play was rewarded with two well-taken goals, showing clinical finishing when opportunities arose. However, defensively, they were exposed at times, especially against Genoa’s direct approach. Cagliari will need to tighten up at the back while maintaining their attacking threat to secure a result.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Genoa's first half performance
halftimeSections.text: Genoa, despite having less possession, were highly effective in attack during the first half. They created more clear-cut chances and were dangerous on the counter, capitalizing on Cagliari’s defensive vulnerabilities to score twice. Their physicality was evident, winning a higher percentage of duels and aerial battles. However, their inability to control the game for sustained periods left them open to Cagliari’s pressure. Genoa will likely look to exploit quick transitions in the second half to edge ahead.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating momentum between the two sides. Cagliari started with a stronger presence, maintaining pressure in the opposition half for extended spells early on. Genoa responded with sharp attacking bursts, gaining traction as the half progressed and striking effectively during key moments. Towards the latter part of the half, Cagliari regained control with consistent forward pushes, but Genoa managed a late surge to keep the game competitive. This back-and-forth dynamic suggests neither team has a clear upper hand heading into the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Cagliari, Sebastiano Esposito and Gennaro Borrelli stood out in the first half with their goal-scoring contributions, posing a constant threat in attack. Marco Palestra also made an impact with a crucial assist, showing creativity in the final third. On Genoa’s side, Vitinha was exceptional, not only scoring but also creating dangerous moments with his playmaking. Leo Østigård’s goal from a set-piece highlighted his aerial prowess, making him a player to watch in both boxes. These individuals could be decisive in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical outlook for second half
halftimeSections.text: Given the open nature of the first half, both teams are likely to adopt an attacking mindset in the second half, unwilling to settle for a draw. Cagliari may focus on leveraging their possession advantage to create more chances, while ensuring their defense is better organized against Genoa’s counters. Genoa, on the other hand, will probably continue targeting quick transitions and set-pieces, areas where they found success. Substitutions could play a key role, with fresh legs potentially tilting the balance in a tightly contested match.
